author: TonyMontana
content:
Hey guys,

since TSLA trade isn't fully over yet I didn't want to do the full trade review for it but I know some of you guys wanted an update.

In terms of TSLA I bought 30 cons (was willing to add up to 50 if it dipped) and the risk was fairly low below the previous consolidation (see pics added) my pt was going to be 360 consolidation break.

However, we had a really nice move same day we bought cons so I ended up selling 10 on first pop (just to lock profits in case of overnight)

and from there i took profits near 370's today for another 10 cons.

I am now sitting on 1/3 of the original position and it still looks good for that move up.

However, the trade itself is risk free now which I always like with swings because now we can hold for that 3rd potential target.

With this being said, I hold 10 cons for tmmrw.

See yall then!

@everyone

author: JLD
content: <@717554586843611217>  <@801169196015943731> I took a trade today on AAPL and lost. I saw that price was slowly working its way higher creating higher lows and then holding the $174.94 line and I decided to enter inon a call.  I ended up getting stopped out quickly. If I would have placed my stop loss lower the trade would have worked out in my favor. My question is was I right to get in on this trade based on what I saw and just made a mistake with my stop position? Or was this not a good set up? I think I see where I should have placed my stop, but am not positive. I only lost 22 dollars on this trade. Playing small while I learn:) Thanks for the input.

author: Jdub
content: Becasue it didn't hold PDL, divergance on the ES and we were also pretty over extended. Whenever we have a strong move to the downside like that majority of the times we will end up having a vshape to target opposing liquidity before we end up moving lower.

author: Aleksandra Seliverstova

author: 👑 of Puts
content: you took short where i went long. My setup is retest though. Opening print is the first 1 min candle so you don't seem to have it marked correctly. I've done backtesting on this setup and in long run it works great. If it's above 1 min opening range i look for call. Below it, i look for puts. As you can see later in the day it reversed and there was a better entry with clear PT.  If you have a reversal setup you need to have clear understanding of it and play it at least from key levels (in the long run reversal never worked for me so i don't play it) But that 481.64 level i had from Wednesday low would have been a good shot at entry for reversal.

author: 👑 of Puts
content: TSLA was great clean retest today! I really think it comes down to backtesting. I do a lot and you're not only getting stats for your setup but also train your visual memory and when you see a good setup you just know to take it and see how it plays out.

author: crunchybrewski
content: In my opinion, there was no trade on GOOG until way late in the day.  My system (very strict) used explicit key levels and uses OBs and FVGs just above or below as confirmation. No levels were retested until something like 2:30 P, when most of the volume has left the market. Technically you can use the double top at 12:45 bc it was testing an OB and showed weakness, but that's not in my system.  The QQQ trade had a beautiful entry a smidge after 10 (where I find the most success). It retested the 5min OR low with confirmation. The peak at 10:30 was nothing but a gap fill to continue lower, so it wouldnt have stopped my system out. As for me, I only saw one entry.

author: demchy19
content:
Hi <@801169196015943731> , <@717554586843611217>  <@1014389514141184074> ,

Could you please share your thoughts on my trade? Today, I was watching TSLA after it pulled back. QQQ was weak, and TSLA wasn’t particularly strong either, showing downside movement (as you can see my TP1 and TP2). My stop loss was set above the 1-minute candle. Initially, I saw a good retest from the 1-minute candle, and I entered the trade when I saw strong price action to the downside. At the time, it was within the 1-minute order block, and the 5-minute chart also looked good for a downside move, with QQQ still showing weakness.

However, TSLA retested the 5-minute candle's open range (OR) -221.12  and pulled back. Once the candle closed above the 1-minute OR, I got stopped out. I entered again with the same thesis, but I got stopped out again.

Could you please let me know if my stop loss was too tight? After that, I had to head to work, and honestly, I was a bit frustrated because after I left, the stock moved in the direction I was expecting. LOL

author: Lauren (lakatrades)
content:
Support Level: Price is holding near $112.00, showing it’s an important support level.
Volume:

There’s higher volume at $112.00 on the 1-minute chart, suggesting buyers might be defending this level.

Daily chart volume is lower than recent days, showing some market hesitation.

Fibonacci Levels:
The lines seem to be Fibonacci levels, and price is testing a key support area.

Candlestick Pattern:
Recent daily candles show indecision (wicks on both sides), indicating a pause in the uptrend after a rally.

Trade Idea:
Bullish Scenario:
If price holds above $112.00 and breaks $113.50, it could be a good long trade opportunity.

Bearish Scenario:
If price falls below $112.00 with higher selling, it could drop further to $111.00 or below.

author: Neto Moreno (Performance Coach)
content:
You have very good feedback from members!
For me it was a good trade, but you needed to be quicker pulling the trigger. Once you touched YDL and then PML, you had to watch closely reaction because if it was a sweep you will bounce hard from there. So what I'd do, and most times do anyways, is take partials on those points and see how the rest of your contracts ride the PA. This could be easily a green trade if you took partials on relevant areas of interest

author: RocketRacer
content:
Hello All,
I am not sure if this is common knowledge but I was hoping to get some confirmation from those in the know. It appears that the farther out of the money you take a contract the larger the % loss you will experience on a drawdown of the underlying stock. For example, I analyzed both of the TSLA trades that Jack and Tony took today and I am not saying I had exactly the same entries and exits but if you analyze the contracts keeping the entry and exit the same I see the following:
For the first trade if you took:
$360 contract = 26% loss
$365 contract = 29% loss
$370 contract = 32% loss
$375 contract = 34% loss
$380 contract = 37% loss
$385 contract = 39% loss
$390 contract = 41% loss
$395 contract = 43% loss
$400 contract = 47% loss
$405 contract = 50% loss

author: Lauren (lakatrades)
content:
Good Morning Sam - 

To answer your question :  Reversal Confirmation:

I observed a strong bullish rejection around the bottom of a clear demand zone where the price had a previous imbalance. The downward trendline broke as a reversal candle formed with a long lower wick, signaling buyers stepping in.
Following the rejection, a small bullish engulfing candle further confirmed momentum shifting to the upside.
I entered the trade where I labeled "Bought Apple Contracts", as the candles began printing higher lows and the downtrend lost steam.

Stop-Loss Placement:

I placed my stop-loss below the demand zone and the wick of the rejection candle. This aligned with key support and offered a good risk-to-reward ratio while protecting against further downside if the reversal failed.

content: I dont think it's a bad trade, we can see it would've worked out today but there were some better setups like AAPL. To me NVDA had room down to to 154 if the prior day's low was broken. By the time the premarket lows were broken on NVDA, most of the meat was off the bone, the easier trade would maybe have been on a rejection of the 1minute opening range <@846172627915505715>

author: Mar
content:
"QQQ: similar to SPY here but this is still holding the 50day SMA so I think going into tomorrow the Q's are going to tell the story of what the markets will do.  IF we bounce off this 50day SMA aggressively and we break the prior day's highs then I think the Q's have room back up 522. ON the other hand if unable to hold the prior day's lows look for the Q's to get down to 506 at least. that is a 4.5 move which is good"

This was a textbook 5min low retest with a nice rejection candle  but my stop was way too tight on this. Look at the impulsive move that broke the 5min level. The stop in this case should have been a candle close ABOVE this impulsive candle that broke the  5min range, I got stopped out just before the flush on the Q's which was a bit frustrating NGL.

author: demchy19
content:
Theses for Tomorrow:
Based on the CPI, the overall market reacted positively and pushed up all day. So, based on this, I’m more focused on being bullish.
-  NVDA is at a very key level. If it breaks above $136.50, we can expect potential upside. However, it’s a really strong resistance level, as it’s also aligned with a trendline. Additionally, QQQ and SPY both look strong right now, which could help NVDA push higher.
-  TSLA is also in a very good position to go long.
-  I also like AAPL—it broke the trendline and moved above the flip zone.
-  MSFT looks good for growth. It broke the trendline and is currently very tight below $427. If it breaks above, it could potentially move higher.
-  The same applies to GOOG.
We’ll see what happens tomorrow!
